assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 20-35 contain a 'Workflow Mode Decision' section that selects 'feature-branch' mode with detailed rationale citing three atomicity indicators: (1) Coordinated schema migration -- migration and code changes are interdependent, (2) Breaking API changes (internal) -- entity layer changes consumed by service/endpoints, (3) Cross-cutting refactor -- removal of advisory_status join touches entity, service, endpoints, ingestion, and tests simultaneously. Concludes: 'No subset of these changes produces a working codebase.'

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\ncreate-branch' (lines 7-8). summary-comment.md confirms it is Task 1 with type 'Bookend (create-branch)' in the task table.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-10-merge-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\nmerge-branch' (lines 7-8). summary-comment.md confirms it is Task 10 (the last task) with type 'Bookend (merge-branch)' in the task table.

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence: All intermediate tasks (2-9) have '## Target Branch\nTC-9005': task-2-create-migration.md (line 5), task-3-update-entity-definitions.md (line 5), task-4-update-advisory-models.md (line 5), task-5-update-advisory-service.md (line 5), task-6-update-advisory-endpoints.md (line 5), task-7-update-ingestion-pipeline.md (line 5), task-8-update-integration-tests.md (line 5), task-9-documentation-updates.md (line 5).

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main

All non-documentation intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency. Documentation tasks may list implementation tasks as dependencies instead of the create-branch bookend task, since documentation depends on implementation completion
assertion_results.evidence: All non-documentation intermediate tasks (2-8) list Task 1 as a dependency: task-2 'Depends on: Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main' (line 44), task-3 (line 58), task-4 (line 40), task-5 (line 43), task-6 (line 43), task-7 (line 49), task-8 (line 48). Documentation task 9 lists implementation tasks (Tasks 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8) as dependencies instead of Task 1 (lines 29-34), which is permitted.

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all non-documentation intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-10-merge-feature-branch.md lists all non-documentation intermediate tasks (2-8) as dependencies: 'Depends on: Task 2', 'Task 3', 'Task 4', 'Task 5', 'Task 6', 'Task 7', 'Task 8' (lines 24-30). It also lists Task 9 (documentation), which does not violate the assertion.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Bookend tasks (non-documentation) are missing required sections. task-1-create-feature-branch.md lacks 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes' sections -- it only has Repository, Target Branch, Bookend Type, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. task-10-merge-feature-branch.md similarly lacks 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes'. Implementation tasks 2-8 all pass with all required sections present. Documentation task 9 passes its exempted requirements (has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements).

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 35: 'The `workflow:feature-branch` label will be applied to the feature issue TC-9005.' Also confirmed in impact-map-comment.md line 35 and summary-comment.md line 30: 'Feature-branch (`TC-9005`)'

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 tasks have separate digest files with valid sha256-md hashes (64 lowercase hex chars each): task-1-digest.md (sha256-md:f2bbe70f...34aa65), task-2-digest.md (sha256-md:a90f1cc4...62386), task-3-digest.md (sha256-md:147ed962...46ce2), task-4-digest.md (sha256-md:4efe961c...f1d41), task-5-digest.md (sha256-md:41ef0e65...ec967), task-6-digest.md (sha256-md:4c2db323...3a257), task-7-digest.md (sha256-md:e50865d5...6acd9), task-8-digest.md (sha256-md:f94658d2...fcb96), task-9-digest.md (sha256-md:2eb99f36...d4d523), task-10-digest.md (sha256-md:bcb1d576...5112a). All hashes verified to be exactly 64 hex characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No evidence of convention-aware enrichment exists in any output file. Grep for 'CONVENTIONS', 'Applies:' across all output files returned no matches related to convention applicability. The only 'Convention' references are 'Conventional Commits format' in docs/constraints.md citations (tasks 2-8 Implementation Notes), which are project constraints, not CONVENTIONS.md conventions. No task file contains an 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' rationale. There is no evidence that convention-applicability validation was performed.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md line 34: '**Priority**: High -- propagated to all created tasks (Feature priority is "High", not "Undefined")'. Lines 42-43 show additional_fields JSON with '"priority": {"name": "High"}' applied to all created tasks.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md line 35: '**Fix Versions**: RHTPA 2.0.0 -- propagated to all created tasks (Feature has fixVersions set; no fixVersion scope restriction in Jira Field Defaults, defaulting to "both")'. Lines 44-45 show additional_fields JSON with '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 2.0.0"}]' applied to all created tasks.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains an 'Inherited Field Values' section (lines 33-35) documenting: 'Priority: High -- propagated to all created tasks' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 2.0.0 -- propagated to all created tasks'. An 'Additional Fields on Created Issues' section (lines 37-47) shows the exact JSON with priority and fixVersions values.
